description_long: This object is a vintage Zenit 12 XP film camera, characterized by its robust, boxy black metal body with textured grip panels on the front and sides. The front face displays the 'ZENIT' branding and model number '12 XP' in white, along with a prominent lens mount and a large, detachable lens cap. The top panel features several mechanical dials and buttons, including a film advance lever, shutter release button, and ISO/ASA setting dial, all rendered in metallic and plastic finishes. The lens itself is cylindrical, protruding from the body, and likely made of glass and metal, with engraved markings for focus and aperture settings. The camera's design is utilitarian, with visible screws and a hot shoe mount for accessories on the top. This type of camera would typically be seen in urban environments, used by photographers or as a collectible item.
description: A vintage Zenit 12 XP film camera with a black, boxy body, metallic dials, and a prominent cylindrical lens with a cap. The camera features textured grips, visible branding, and mechanical controls.
description_view_0: The rear and top of the camera are visible, showing the viewfinder, film advance lever, and control dials.
description_view_1: The left side of the camera is shown, highlighting the textured grip, side profile of the lens, and top dials.
description_view_2: The front view displays the 'ZENIT' branding, model number, lens with cap, and the main control interface.
description_view_3: The right side of the camera is visible, showing the grip, side of the lens, and additional control dials.
